title: An improved medical leg support arrangement to aid in the healing and strengthening of damaged, injured and/or replaced bone, muscle and/or tissue of the leg
abstract: A medical leg support arrangement including front and rear sections having arched-up distal ends wherein a plurality of ground-engaging wheels supported on opposing sides of each of the front section and rear section including arched-up distal ends of the front section and rear section such that when in use weight-bearing support of the arrangement is maintained upon initial contact and departure of the arrangement from the ground so as to allow continuous support to the leg of the user during back and forth circumferential motion and wherein a motion sensor arrangement is adapted to sense the back and forth circumferential motion of the leg of the user between flexion and extension.
claims:
Claims 1. A medical leg support arrangement adapted to increase a circular range of motion of a leg to aid in the healing and strengthening of damaged, injured and/or replaced bone, muscle and/or tissue of the leg, said arrangement including; a main housing assembly including a fastening means to retain a foot of a user upon an upper surface of the main housing assembly; a front section of the main housing assembly further including a curved and/or arched-up distal end and wherein said a rear section of the main housing assembly further includes a curved and/or arched-up distal end; a plurality of ground-engaging wheels supported on opposing sides of each of the front section and rear section, wherein the plurality of rotatable ground- engaging wheels are aligned substantially longitudinal along the main housing assembly including at or near the end of each of the respective curved and/or arched-up distal ends of the front section and rear section of the main housing assembly so as to provide for a generally curved circumferential alignment of the plurality of ground-engaging wheels on each of the opposing sides of the front section and rear section; such that when in use weight-bearing support of the arrangement is maintained upon initial contact and departure of the arrangement from the ground so as to allow continuous support to the leg of the user during back and forth circumferential motion; and a motion sensor arrangement adapted to sense said back and forth circumferential motion of the leg of the user between flexion and extension. 2. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 1 wherein a controller working with said motion sensor arrangement is adapted to sense said back 

 and forth circumferential motion of the leg of the user between flexion and extension to which the medical leg support arrangement has been positioned thereon, wherein the controller and the motion sensor arrangement are in communication such that said back and forth circumferential motion of the leg of the user between flexion and extension can be monitored, recorded, displayed and/or used as a visual aid for the user undergoing the requisite healing and strengthening. 3. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 2 wherein the motion sensor arrangement includes a sensor fastenable to the leg of the user to which the medical leg support arrangement has been positioned thereon. 4. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 3 wherein the motion sensor arrangement includes at least a pair of sensors, wherein a first sensor is generally fastenable to the upper leg of the user to which the medical leg support arrangement has been positioned thereon and a second sensor is generally fastenable to the lower leg of the user to which the medical leg support arrangement has been positioned thereon. 5. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 3 wherein the sensor fastenable to the leg of the user to which the medical leg support arrangement has been positioned thereon further includes functionality of a pedometer. 6. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 3 or 5 wherein the sensor can be worn on a wrist of the user when not fastened to the leg of the user to which the medical leg support arrangement has been positioned thereon. 7. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 6 wherein the sensor includes smart technology providing a display, wherein said display is interfaceable with the user to provide information as to the user activity, wherein said information is recordable. 8. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 7 wherein the medical leg support arrangement further includes a dynamometer communicating with a dynamometer controller to measure force, strength, power and/or loading applied by the leg of the user to which the medical leg support arrangement has been positioned thereon. 9. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 8 wherein the dynamometer arrangement is in communication with the motion sensor arrangement such that the user is able to in real time be visually made aware upon said display, the angulation of the leg of the user to which the medical leg support arrangement has been positioned thereon between flexion and full extension as well as a recognition of the self-loading or strength being applied by the user during back and forth circumferential motion of the leg of the user between flexion and extension. 10. A medical leg support arrangement of anyone of claims 1 to 9 wherein the main housing assembly includes a separable and independent front section and a rear section, wherein said separable and independent front section and said rear section are adjustably mountable and moveable upon a central strut such that when the separable and independent front section and the rear section of the main housing assembly have been adjusted and fixed upon the central strut, an upper surface on the front section and a corresponding upper surface on the rear section define a platform on which a foot of a user would be restable thereon and a fastening arrangement to retain said foot upon the defined platform. 11. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 10 wherein each of the front section and rear section includes a plurality of channels that provide corresponding slots. 12. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 11 wherein each slot is adapted to receive a shaft mountable and supportable within the front section or 

 rear section of the main housing assembly wherein the ground-engaging wheels are fixable and rotatable there upon said shaft when mounted. 13. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 12 wherein each slot runs across the breadth of the front section or rear section so that a pair of ground- engaging wheels can be positioned on either side of the respective front section or rear section of the main housing assembly upon a single shaft. 14. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 13 wherein each slot is configured towards or at the bottom edge, base or under-side of each of the front section or rear section of the main housing assembly. 15. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 10 wherein at an apex or tip of the curved and/or arched-up end of the respective front section and rear section of the main housing assembly, includes a hook member wherein a cord is adaptable to be engageable with the hook. 16. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 10 wherein each of the front section and rear section of the main housing assembly are separate single moulds. 17. The medical leg support arrangement of claim 10 wherein the fastening arrangement includes a pair opposing brackets on each side of the front section wherein an adjustable strip or resilient tape and/or lace is engageable thereto said pair of opposing brackets.
